rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (2/10) and rest on the same job-relevant reasoning: vast overqualification for a junior role, basic-only German failing the strong German requirement, and a recent tech focus on TypeScript/Node.js/DLang rather than Java. The differences are purely cosmetic rewording (e.g., 'over 15 years' vs 'nearly two decades', 'him' vs 'them') and fall within ordinary run-to-run noise. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change from Bogdan Szabo to Mohammed Al-Said.
